openQuestions.question: Q1 (BLOCKING) Stage-model mapping: prototype 6-stage board vs HEAD's separate status unions; are queued/scheduled persisted statuses or derived views?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Derived view over existing statuses (quick-win), no schema change, single advance() routed through existing validateTransition; sn-content-pipeline-3 only if server-derived.

openQuestions.question: Q2 Where do exports live (pipeline 5×CSV/JSON menu, brief PDF/MD/JSON, post md/html/pdf)?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep existing endpoints behind an overflow menu on the new Toolbar — zero server work, zero capability loss.

openQuestions.question: Q3 Client-request production lifecycle (deliver-URL/notes, upgrade path, resubmit, post_review handoff): Inbox, pipeline drawer, or both?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Pipeline drawer keeps production actions; Inbox keeps triage — split by actor.

openQuestions.question: Q4 Matrix depth: keep CellDetailPanel + 8 cell statuses + template/matrix edit/delete/duplicate, or simplify to prototype grid?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: T1 carry-over: existing MatrixGrid/CellDetailPanel/TemplateEditor/MatrixBuilder behind the Matrix mode entry.

openQuestions.question: Q5 Cannibalization warnings + growth-tier gate: where in new IA, and does tier gating survive on this surface?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Content Health tab section + board-card attn treatment; keep tierAtLeast gate.

openQuestions.question: Q6 Content Health dedupe: pipeline Content Health tab vs seo-audit ContentDecay (?sub=content-decay) — one home or two?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Pipeline becomes the acting home (queue-refresh); seo-audit keeps the diagnostic view; decay-alert deep-link retargets to pipeline.

openQuestions.question: Q7 Calendar interactivity: confirm schedule-a-draft, unschedule, AI suggest-dates carry into Calendar mode, and whether briefs/requests/matrix items stay plottable.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Carry all three interactions (endpoints exist); keep all 4 item types; drop only 'created'-date plotting as an explicit trade-off row.

openQuestions.question: Q8 Post-editor power features (versions/revert, section regenerate, AI-fix diff apply, unification badge, publish-with-image): confirm homes in draft workspace.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: All live in the draft-workspace side rail; gate 'Resolve' wires to the ai-fix diff apply.

openQuestions.question: Q9 Voice score: does numeric score + feedback + re-score become the Brand-voice gate detail view?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Yes — gate expands to show MetricRing score, prose feedback, Re-score button.

openQuestions.question: Q10 Suggested-brief snooze (1w/1m): intake card menu or deliberate drop?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep — overflow menu on intake cards (status union already has 'snoozed').

openQuestions.question: Q11 Generation style (standard/concise/hybrid): keep as advanced control in brief/draft workspaces?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep behind an 'Advanced' disclosure in both workspaces.

openQuestions.question: Q12 API-only endpoints with no UI caller (validate-keyword(s) #30, recommend-keywords #69, matrix cannibalization #70): confirm actual consumers before treating as UI-droppable.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Grep MCP tools + server-internal callers during build ticket; do not delete endpoints in this rebuild either way.

openQuestions.question: Q13 New proposals N1-N10 each need explicit sign-off (esp. N4 engagement data, N5 graduate contract, N7 questionnaire).
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Adopt N1/N2/N3/N4-core/N6/N8 in rebuild; defer N4-engagement/N5/N7-questionnaire/N9/N10 to C3-style follow-ups.

openQuestions.question: NEW: sparkline identity — Published-tab trend is per-request today (content-requests.ts:305) but the tab renders posts; posts without a linked request have no trend source. Accept sparkline-only-when-linked, or build post-URL-keyed trend (sn-content-pipeline-2)?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Ship verdict+tiles first; sparkline behind sn-content-pipeline-2, absent state until then (no fabricated series).

composition.statesPlan: Per-mode 4-state: Skeleton loading, action-CTA EmptyState, ErrorState+retry (incl. brief-not-found recovery), plus locked states for missing publish target and growth-tier gate; generating/error render as board-card states.
composition.mountNote: Mounts as Page 'content-pipeline' inside RebuiltAppChrome behind ui-rebuild-shell; legacy aliases content/calendar/seo-briefs/subscriptions and ?tab= modes must keep resolving (D8 redirect map + tab-deep-link contract test).

crossSurfaceDeps: content-perf surface owns the Published-tab fold (ContentPerformance → Published); sn-content-pipeline-1/-2 shared with it
seo-audit surface: ContentDecay dedupe (Q6) — decay alert deep-link currently targets seo-audit ?sub=content-decay
inbox/requests surface: RequestManager moved to Inbox but per-request production actions live here (Q3 split)
keywords surface + D3 (SEO Editor Research mode): fixContext prefill senders + N9 keyword picker data
workspace settings owns Webflow publish-target config that usePublishTarget reads
strategy/issue-lenses: intake strategy source + ?tab=briefs|posts deep-links + rec↔pipeline reconciliation
unknowns: Guide element (#11): doc says Parity Ledger claims at:'guide' but none found in pipeline.js — verify at build; classified defer-candidate on doc evidence only
Subscription drawer notes-field editability (#73) — doc flags 'verify', not re-verified against prototype js here
Actual consumers of validate-keyword(s)/recommend-keywords/matrix-cannibalization endpoints (#30/#69/#70) — MCP/server-internal usage not exhaustively traced (Q12)
Whether the per-request trend endpoint covers enough published posts (post↔request linkage rate) to make sn-content-pipeline-2 optional — not measurable statically

verdicts.evidence: shared/types/outcome-tracking.ts:471-496 (OutcomeReadback) has fields limited to score/position/clicks — no time-on-page or engagement field (grep engagement|averageSessionDuration|engagementRate over the file = empty). enrichPostsWithOutcomes (server/content-posts-db.ts:325-343, wired at server/routes/content-posts.ts:136) only attaches the scalar `outcome` readback. GA4 per-page engagement fetchers do exist (server/google-analytics.ts:194 averageSessionDuration, 257 pagePath dim, 648/675 engagementRate) but are not joined to posts. Partial is correct; effort M is reasonable (readback interface extension + new GA4-per-post join, no migration required since it's a read-side decoration).
verdicts.correction: Minor: the claim cites GA4 lines 646-675 as 'pagePath + engagementRate' — line 675's engagementRate lives in the ORGANIC-summary fetcher (not per-page); the per-pagePath fetcher getGA4TopPages (line ~257) returns userEngagementDuration, not engagementRate. The engagement data needed per-post would come from a pagePath-filtered report, which the codebase supports (server/google-analytics.ts:509-540 pagePath CONTAINS filter) but does not currently expose keyed by post.

verdicts.evidence: OutcomeReadback carries only baseline/current scalars — no series field (confirmed shared/types/outcome-tracking.ts:471-496; grep sparkline|timeseries|series = empty). A per-page GSC daily-clicks trend endpoint EXISTS and is reusable: GET /api/content-performance/:workspaceId/:requestId/trend (server/routes/content-requests.ts:305-334) resolves request.targetPageSlug → full page URL → getPageTrend(...,90) (server/search-console.ts:766). getPageTrend is keyed by PAGE URL, not by request-id per se — a published post also has a target page URL, so the sparkline series is a page-URL lookup that already exists, not net-new plumbing.
verdicts.correction: Two detail corrections. (1) whereItLives is wrong: the trend endpoint lives in server/routes/content-requests.ts:305 calling server/search-console.ts:getPageTrend — NOT in server/domains/content/content-performance.ts (that module backs the non-trend /content-performance summary). (2) 'nothing keyed by post' overstates the gap: the trend is keyed by page URL derived from a content-request's targetPageSlug; extending to posts is a slug/URL-resolution wire-up over an existing series fetcher, so effort is closer to S–M than M.

verdicts.evidence: No persisted queued/scheduled content pipeline stage: shared/types/content.ts:272-277 documents plannedPublishAt as 'intent' only. The only lifecycleStage derivation in the codebase is for the Keywords Command Center (deriveLifecycleStage in server/domains/keyword-command-center/read-model.ts, typed KeywordLifecycleStage) — there is NO analogous derivation over posts+requests+briefs (grep lifecycleStage|pipelineStage across server/src/shared returns only KCC + keywords-rebuilt UI). Post/request/matrix statuses live in separate state-machine unions (server/state-machines.ts). existsToday=no is correct; kind=derivation and effort=M are sound.

The evidence 'only ranking_opportunity is generated today' is false. A decay-sourced suggested-brief WRITE PATH already exists and ships in prod: POST /api/content-decay/:workspaceId/analyze (server/routes/content-decay.ts:22) runs fireBridge('bridge-decay-suggested-brief') which loops analysis.decayingPages.slice(0,5) and calls createSuggestedBrief({... source: 'content_decay' ...}) (server/routes/content-decay.ts:38-50), broadcasting WS_EVENTS.SUGGESTED_BRIEF_UPDATED. Additionally the store's createSuggestedBrief default source is already 'content_decay' (server/suggested-briefs-store.ts:113,133,150). So both the store default AND a live decay→brief seam exist.
verdicts.correction: The N6 'Queue refresh' one-card intake is a THIN wrapper over an existing seam, not net-new source support. What is genuinely absent is only the specific queueRefresh HTTP entry point (grep queueRefresh|queue.?refresh = empty) — the createSuggestedBrief({source:'content_decay'}) call and its broadcast already exist and are reusable. This makes effort=S accurate but for a different reason (reuse the existing bridge/store path, don't build source generation). existsToday should read 'partial (write path already exists; only a dedicated intake endpoint/trigger is missing)', and evidence must be corrected to cite server/routes/content-decay.ts:38-50 as the existing decay-sourced brief writer.

verdicts.evidence: The remind pattern exists: POST /api/deliverables/:workspaceId/:id/remind (server/routes/deliverables.ts:274-289) calls remindDeliverable + addActivity('deliverable_reminded'). No brief/content-request nudge endpoint exists (grep nudge|remind over server/routes/content-requests.ts, content-briefs.ts, content-posts.ts = empty). Partial and effort=S are correct; the deliverables remind handler is a clean template.

src/components/ContentPlanner.tsx:88-107 handleBulkAction only branches on export_csv and send_review; the trailing comment 'Other bulk actions will be wired to specific endpoints as they're implemented' confirms generate_briefs is declared-but-no-op. MatrixGrid.tsx wires the 'Generate Briefs' menu item to onBulkAction('generate_briefs', ...) but no server endpoint receives it (grep generate_briefs across server/ = empty; only client references). existsToday=no, kind=job, effort=M all sound.

grep 'graduate' across server/ + src/ hits only server/roi.ts, src/utils/formatNumbers.ts, and src/components/client/the-issue/outcomeProvenance.ts — all provenance/ROI language, none an insight-creation write. No 'graduate to Insights Engine' seam from a published win exists. existsToday=no is correct; contract is genuinely undefined.

No content-brief questionnaire path exists (grep 'questionnaire' in server/routes/content-briefs.ts + content-requests.ts = NONE). existsToday=no for the content-brief-specific flow is correct. HOWEVER a full client-facing questionnaire flow DOES exist and is directly reusable as a template: src/components/client/ClientOnboardingQuestionnaire.tsx (full-screen multi-step client portal flow) + POST public onboarding submission in server/routes/public-portal.ts that transforms responses into KB/brand-voice/personas, plus the brandscript questionnaire-seeded path (server/brandscript.ts).
verdicts.correction: existsToday should be nuanced from a flat 'no' to 'no for the content-brief 5-Q flow, but a client-portal questionnaire submit+transform pattern already exists (ClientOnboardingQuestionnaire + public-portal onboarding submission) to model on'. This does not lower effort materially (still L: new AI operations + brief-answer folding + a new client surface) but it removes the 'net-new client-facing surface from scratch' framing — the client questionnaire scaffolding is reusable.

verdicts.evidence: regenerate-outline WITH feedback exists as an async job endpoint: POST /api/content-briefs/:workspaceId/:briefId/regenerate-outline (server/routes/content-briefs.ts:213-225) → startContentBriefRegenerateJob({mode:'outline'}). No per-field assist endpoints for angle/keywords/links/eeat exist (grep 'assist' in content-briefs.ts = empty; eeatGuidance appears only as a brief schema field at line 12/69, not an assist operation). Partial + effort=M are correct: outline maps to the existing job, the other four fields are net-new named AI operations.
